---
title: "Computational Paper Guidelines"
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    toc: false
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 2
    grid:
        sidebar-width: 200px
        body-width: 1500px
    include-in-header: header.html

---

## FAIR Data

---

### Level 1
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
# Data available in an accessible permanent repository (DOI)

- Zenodo
- other institutional repositories (find examples)
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
# Free license (e.g., Creative Commons)
The License must be clearly indicated in the root of the repository (**LICENSE/COPYING**).  
Exemple of valid licenses:

- **[CC-BY-4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)** — Attribution; for data/docs; FAIR-compliant openness.

- refine possibilities in CC-BY
:::

- File listing authors and contributors

### Level 2
- Open file formats (e.g., HDF5, NetCDF)  
- Complete metadata file  
- Documentation  

### Level 3
- Curation and validation ensuring consistency between content and paper  



## FAIR Software

### Level 1

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Code hosted on a forge with version, commit number or Zenodo DOI

#### `git` forges:

- **[GitLab](https://gitlab.com)** — Open-core; self-host or cloud; good CI/CD; FAIR-aligned transparency.
- **[GitHub](https://github.com)** — Widely used; strong integration tools; limited openness (proprietary).
- **[Codeberg](https://codeberg.org)** — Fully open-source (Gitea-based); privacy-friendly; EU-hosted.
- **[SourceHut](https://sr.ht)** — Minimalist; open protocols; plain-text workflows; fully libre.
- **[Bitbucket](https://bitbucket.org)** — Proprietary; integrates with Atlassian suite; less open.
- **[Gitea](https://gitea.io)** — Lightweight, self-hosted, MIT-licensed; supports FAIR openness.
- **[Forgejo](https://forgejo.org)** — Gitea fork; community-driven; strong open governance.
- **[GRICAD GitLab](https://gricad-gitlab.univ-grenoble-alpes.fr/)** — Academic research-forge (Université Grenoble Alpes + CNRS + INRIA); local GitLab instance for research software. - **[IRD Forge](https://forge.ird.fr/)** — Institutional GitLab-based forge by Institut de recherche pour le développement (IRD); supports versioning, CI/CD, public/private code. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
- **[Forgejo / Fordj (France-specific interest)](https://code.gouv.fr/fr/bluehats/autres-forges/)** — Libre community-forge (via DINUM / BlueHats) for French public sector; open-source development. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

#### Getting version / git commit:

- [Git commit](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Basics-Viewing-the-Commit-History)
- [GitHub commit](https://docs.github.com/en/pull-requests/committing-changes-to-your-project/creating-and-editing-commits/about-commits)
- [Gilab commit](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/project/repository/commits/)
- [Zenodo DOI, code archive](https://help.zenodo.org/docs/github/archive-software/github-upload/)

#### Citation

- README file: Doe, J., & Smith, A. (2025). *CoolSim: Climate simulation toolkit* (Version 2.3.1) [Computer software]. GitHub. Commit: 4f8a9d2. https://github.com/exampleuser/coolsim/tree/4f8a9d2

- As a bibtex entry:
```bib
@software{Doe_CoolSim_2025,
  author       = {Doe, John and Smith, Alice},
  title        = {CoolSim: Climate simulation toolkit},
  year         = {2025},
  version      = {2.3.1},
  url          = {https://github.com/exampleuser/coolsim/tree/4f8a9d2},
  note         = {Commit 4f8a9d2b6e7c4e1a52d9e9e2f3c17b6a1d3f45ac},
}
```
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Free license

The License must be clearly indicated in the root of the repository (**LICENSE/COPYING**).
Exemple of valid licenses:

- **[MIT License](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)** — Permissive; free reuse, modify, distribute; attribution only.
- **[Apache 2.0](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)** — Permissive; attribution + patent grant; high interoperability.
- **[BSD 3-Clause](https://opensource.org/licenses/BSD-3-Clause)** — Permissive; attribution + non-endorsement.
- **[GPL-3.0](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html)** — Copyleft; derivatives must remain open.
- **[LGPL-3.0](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/lgpl-3.0.en.html)** — Copyleft; Lesser GPL permits use of the library in proprietary programs.
- **[MPL-2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/)** — Weak copyleft; file-level openness; flexible reuse.
- **[CC-BY-4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)** — Attribution; for data/docs; FAIR-compliant openness.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Authors and contributors file

The authors and contributors should be given with one or several of the following:

- **`AUTHORS` file** — Plain text or Markdown file listing main authors and maintainers.
  Example: `AUTHORS`, `AUTHORS.md`

- **`CONTRIBUTORS` file** — Lists additional contributors or institutional credits.
  Example: `CONTRIBUTORS`, `CONTRIBUTORS.md`

- Within **`README` file** — Optional “Authors” or “Credits” section; human-readable summary.

- Within **Version control management** — With clean commit history, the exact contributor list is metadata.

:::

### Level 2
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Documented code

There is API documentation (inline annotations) and a user/developper documentation.

- [Doxygen](https://www.doxygen.nl)
- [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org) and [pydoc](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pydoc.html)
- [JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app)
- [Documenter.jl](https://juliadocs.github.io/Documenter.jl)
- [rustdoc](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustdoc/) and [Cargo doc]( https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/commands/cargo-doc.html)
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# “How to use it?”

- instructions
- README
- example scripts
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Metadata file
:::

### Level 3
::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Examples
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Automated tests
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
# Functionality documentation (e.g., API documentation)
:::

## Reproducible Results

### Level 1
- Persistent identifier (commit, SWHID, DOI, etc.)
- Reproducibility recipe (code execution + post-processing)
- README and scripts 

### Level 2
- Full hardware/software environment (Guix, venv, requirements.txt, renv, etc.)
- Workflow manager for reproducibility
